name: L. W. FARRAR
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield

name: E. A. HOOD
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield

name: J. B. HIBBITTS
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield

name: C. O. BROWN
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield

name: FRED J. DIETERLE
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield
description: This is the guy we call "Dutch," Who doesn't amount to much! He's loud, and—oh, well, He's good at a yell, And singing and preaching and such.

name: WILLIAM BURNEY FLOYD
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield
description: He has always pitied the weakness of his adversaries so much that his victories have been occasions of sorrow.

name: ANDREW L. LANTZ
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield
description: We have a lot of dreadnoughts and guns, But this is our only Lantz! The girls think he's of the chivalrous ones—So we haven't the ghost of a chance, Because of Lantz.

name: FAYETTE ARTHUR BOTTS
motto: "And still the wonder grew that one small head could carry all he knew." —Vicar of Wakefield
description: I am the very "bone" of Jewell, gifted with a natural antipathy for mathematics and the natural sciences.

name: Artie Teller Newman
description: Don't tread on me—or my hat.

name: Virgil J. Newport
description: Won a "J" in Football, so nothing else can keep him in Anxiety, unless it be Chemistry.

name: Hurschel L. Caldwell
description: I wonder if this world is real, Or these bright things we see Just spread about to make us feel The joy that is to be.

name: Allen Luther Hendrix
description: I wish the professors wouldn't ask me so often for information. It makes me blush to have them think I know more than they.

name: Guy Vaughn Price
clubs: Second Place State I. P. A. Oratorical '15
description: He elucidates his ponderous cogitations in a most heterogeneous array of polysyllabic verbalities.

name: Grover Cleveland Brenneman
description: O World, canst thou stand this man when thou believest "A thing of beauty is a joy forever?"

name: Paul Lucas Jones
description: At spreading B——well, you know— My record is just so-so! But while you're in the picture show Just watch me fight the piano.

name: Frank H. Davidson
description: I don't believe in resorting to schemes—not even in Physics.

name: EARL H. DANLEY
description: I am really surprised to find that I know so much. Well, I guess it takes a great man a long time to find himself.

name: REQUA W. BELL
description: He believes heartily in showing due consideration to all athletic opponents.

name: CARLYLE CAMPBELL
description: "Chirp" is a firm believer in Chautauquas and girls in general, and both in particular.

name: WILLIAM JAMES CHASE
description: He's plain and earnest of face, A very good singer of bass! No Nimrod is he, But still one can see He thinks a lot of the Chase.

name: REX IDRIS JAMES
description: He nibbled at the mental bait Until he bared the hook! The profs have jerked and jerked—but wait, Here goes he from their brook.

name: LOUIS H. HIBBITTS
description: If he wants to use his brother for a handy reference library, what is there proHibbitts?

name: JOHN DANIEL KERN
description: This somnolent fellow called Kern Hath a nature of very odd turn, And it seems that he doesn't give a "dern."

name: WILLIAM RAYMOND BALLINGER
description: A mathematician and a strategist in love, who has waged a long and anxious combat with Cupid: Result uncertain.

name: ERMY CLEO MARET
description: All the problems we used to solve concerning the revolutions of a grindstone must have been based on fallacies. We've been watching this one and he hasn't revolved.

name: JULIUS ROBERT MANTEY
clubs: President K. C. Student Volunteer Union '15-'16
description: I am Jewell's great Julius, nothing undervalued to Rome's great Julius in my own estimation, though I look more like Napoleon. Great in either case.

name: ERSKINE A. FRANKLIN
description: He does not believe in preparedness or safety first.

name: WALTER IFLAND WELDEN
description: I have made a marvelous invention Which produces real osseous tissue, And without right serious intention Myself am become its first issue.

name: J. R. HIBBITTS, Jr.
description: Look him well o'er and you shall find In science only he is a \"grind.\"

name: J. H. RAMSBOTTOM
description: Too well I know that life is but a waking dream; hence shall I take me back to the farm, where I may dream in peace.

name: DAVID M. TROUT
description: This poor \"fish\" has floundered along till he's through college. This is the miracle of the fish, and we hope he will furnish food for five thousand.

name: RICHARD O. SHANNON
description: \"Dick\" is our Ladies' Man. Now that he is through we can say we hope he'll make a better impression on them than he has on us.

name: Potter, A. B.
description: He's very important—even the street cars could not run through Liberty without him.

name: Leonard, Benjamin Thomas
description: Got run over by "Slude" Moorman on the football field, and ever since has been accounted a great athlete.

name: Wilkinson, Austin Tecumseh
description: They've moved away the barber shops And hid my old razor! But I've advanced by flips and flops And slid the college through! So now for all us Senior "fops" I bid you all Adieu.

name: Powell, E. J.
description: He's quite a famous Lecturer, sure, This man of tragic scowl! But for his fame he needs a cure— Perhaps needs less of Powell.

name: Miller, Edward Vincent
description: I am a son of "Old Kentuck" (You'd know that, anyway)! From hot-shot questions I can duck And sometimes get away.

name: Harrel, Chastain Gaint
description: Wrote a 12,000-word essay in Sociology all in vain.

name: Davis, Arthur Lowell
description: I will give you the chemico-physico-mathematical formula for anything but making love.

name: Brown, Carl O.
motto: "Speaks an infinite deal of nothing."

name: GEORGE CLYDE FAULKNER
description: I have moved among my fellows as an enigma—a veritable shuttlecock playing between wisdom and folly.

name: ROY THOMAS MARR
description: We shall not do him slander but true praise: He's noted for his odd and boisterous ways.

name: RAYMOND H. EWING
description: Lo! I have made a bargain with the Lord; He hath helped me on the gridiron and I shall help him on the mission field.

name: CLIFTON ORNAL STOUT
description: I don't believe any man should take his full number of hours. At last I've convinced Dr. Fleet that he shouldn't—be so strict.

name: DAVID WILLIAM JONES
description: I really wonder if the boys Do pay attention to my noise, For reason forces me to think I am an obscure bee, by jink. But handsome is as handsome does, So round about their heads I buzz.

name: JOHN BERTWELL EWING
description: And now I'm through, and I wonder if I've really earned my degree or more than dipped the surface of scholastic life.

name: EMMET H. HOOD
description: Gone, but not forgotten.

name: W. M. BENNETT
description: To prove that I know my own mind I have betaken myself back to the haunts of Gara'to.

name: PORTER M. WALDRON
description: He's very much given to interurban flirtations and is known as a protector of the fair sex.

name: LYNN WOODARD FARRAR
description: This Senior of the red mustache, Doth of his opponents make hash! If they ever scorn to yield When he meets 'em on the field.

name: CLYDE C. CHURCH
description: This is the great religious problem—the Church going to the dogs.

name: MANLIUS T. HANCOCK
description: "Empty" in name and fact, though a ladies' man.

name: CHARLES ELMER TURNER
motto: "It's a matter of indifference to me."

name: FREDERICK ALLEN SYKES
motto: "I know what it is to be steam-rollered."

name: ALAN F. WHERRITT
motto: "Ambition draws him toward the bar of justice."

name: HARRY ROGERS
description: He has something of the fictitious in him.

name: WILLIAM H. LOBER
motto: "I dearly love the Parkereochemicality of science."

name: CHARLES R. SCARBROUGH
description: is a dealer in modern sees and ancient saws.

name: HARRY A. CLINE
description: He lures the women with his operatic voice.

name: LEE R. RALSTON
description: By his solemnity you can see he is deeply in love.

name: FORREST EDWIN LONG
description: Noted for his characteristic of modesty.

name: MAYNARD HALL THORNE
description: He sees every piece of bread we throw.

name: JAMES NOAH FORD
description: Was not responsible for what the peace party did.

name: VIRGIL JONES SIMS
description: He's too bashful to succeed with the fair sex.

name: JAMES W. HERRING
description: swims so far beneath the surface that we can't tell whether he's a whale or a minnow.

name: ARTHUR CRAFTON TUTT
motto: "Ambition should be made of sterner stuff."

name: JAMES EWART BELL
motto: "Dr. Greene, Dr. Cook, and I—and the college."

name: THOMAS E. BROCKHOUSE
description: Lives up to the name of his home town.

name: RIDER LARRABEE RICHMOND
description: "Chink" marks the bloom of a fresh cycle of wit.

name: S. S. MAJOR
description: A firm believer in majority rule—for all classes.

name: KENNETH HARRY PARKER
description: An experienced faker of faces.

name: NOVUS HOMO EST REED
description: Fiddles around more than any other "guy" in school.

name: HAL C. HEAD
description: As editor of the Student, Hal has been one of the chief agents of the "preparedness" agitation.

name: ELDONE HAMILTON TRUEX
description: To him the music of the spheres sounds base.

name: HARRISON WILLIAMS GILL
motto: "My actions belie my deep, artistic thoughts."

name: EDWARD M. LANDS
motto: "I am never quite sure that I have the point."

name: NOAH W. HUNT
description: Destined to be one of the most brilliant scholars of his day.

name: JOHN A. PITTS
description: Believes implicitly in the futility of effort.

name: WALTER S. KOONS
motto: "I am an Edison and a Carnegie combined."

name: GILBERT F. ANTOINE
description: A silent, smiling little man, he goes his way.

name: PAUL HUBBARD
description: Endeavors to keep constantly in the limelight.

name: ROY H. PARKER
description: His speech is voluble, voluminous and voluptuous.

name: JOHN A. BRODY
description: As tough and crooked as an Ozark sapling.

name: IRA C. WOLFE
description: We admire his social taste. Just turn the page and see the bunch he left behind.

name: WILL JOHN DIEGELMAN
description: Perhaps I can't tell why, but I have my convictions on the subject.

name: EDWARD NELSON TUTT
description: Tutt, Tutt. He is not so honory as his brother.

name: OTTO ROTHWELL
description: Has learned to labor (in Chem.) and to wait (tables).

name: LEE SUMNER CONNER
motto: "It is my business to be quick, versatile and superficial."

name: BENJAMIN FRANKLIN GRAY
motto: "All, my musings are secret and profound."

name: I. R. MORRISON
motto: "'Ugly' is a sure-enough shark at getting by the Profs."

name: RILEY E. SNOW
description: He makes us all think of cold feet.

name: JAMES ROBERSON HALLIBURTON
description: When William Jewell gets to be Co-ed, "Cockle Burr" wants to be president of it.
